module_functions.name: run_command_safely
module_functions.signature: run_command_safely(cmd_args: list[str], description: str, capture_output: bool, check: bool) -> tuple[Tuple[bool, Optional[str], Optional[str]]]
module_functions.docstring:
Run a command safely using subprocess with argument lists

    Args:
        cmd_args: List of command arguments (e.g., ["gh", "pr", "create", "--title", "My PR"])
        description: Human-readable description of what the command does
        capture_output: Whether to capture stdout/stderr
        check: Whether to raise CalledProcessError on non-zero exit code

    Returns:
        Tuple of (success, stdout, stderr)

module_functions.name: create_github_pr
module_functions.signature: create_github_pr(title: str, body: str, base: str, head: Optional[str]) -> bool
module_functions.docstring:
Create GitHub PR without dquote issues

    Args:
        title: PR title
        body: PR description
        base: Base branch (default: develop)
        head: Head branch (default: current branch)

    Returns:
        True if successful, False otherwise

module_functions.name: git_commit_safely
module_functions.docstring:
Commit changes without dquote issues

    Args:
        message: Commit message

    Returns:
        True if successful, False otherwise

module_functions.name: git_push_safely
module_functions.docstring:
Push changes without dquote issues

    Args:
        remote: Remote name (default: origin)
        branch: Branch name (default: current branch)

    Returns:
        True if successful, False otherwise

module_functions.name: git_add_safely
module_functions.docstring:
Add files to git without dquote issues

    Args:
        files: File path(s) to add

    Returns:
        True if successful, False otherwise

module_functions.name: docker_run_safely
module_functions.signature: docker_run_safely(image: str, env_vars: Optional[dict], volumes: Optional[list[str]], ports: Optional[list[str]], command: Optional[list[str]]) -> bool
module_functions.docstring:
Run Docker container without dquote issues

    Args:
        image: Docker image name
        env_vars: Environment variables dict
        volumes: Volume mappings list
        ports: Port mappings list
        command: Command to run in container

    Returns:
        True if successful, False otherwise

module_functions.name: quick_recovery_script
module_functions.docstring: Quick recovery script template for dquote issues

module_docstring:
Safe Shell Runner

This module provides safe alternatives to shell commands that commonly cause dquote issues.
Uses Python subprocess with argument lists instead of shell string construction.
